S4-1.evidence: CrashReportActivity.kt:84-85 — window.setFlags(FLAG_SECURE, FLAG_SECURE) called in onCreate after super.onCreate() and before setContent. Import of android.view.WindowManager added. Correct ordering: flag is applied before any composition, so Recents thumbnails and OEM capture collectors will see a blank surface from the first frame. Using setFlags with matching mask+value is idiomatic; addFlags would also work, setFlags is equivalent here.

S4-3.evidence: CrashReportActivity.kt:111 and :114 — both Toast.makeText calls now pass applicationContext instead of this. Prevents the Toast from holding an Activity reference past finish(), eliminating the short-lived leak and matching the standard Android guidance for toasts fired from short-lived activities.

S4-2.evidence: tasks/4.md line 150 explicitly accepts rotation/cancellation behaviour on the crash screen as 'приемлемо (редкий кейс)'. Confirmed the failure mode is ergonomic, not a security or integrity issue: the SAF write (LogExportManager.writeTo) has already committed bytes to the user-chosen Uri before File.delete() runs, so user data is preserved regardless of whether lifecycleScope cancels between writeTo and delete. Worst case: stale crash.log stays in filesDir for one cold-start cycle; on next launch the banner reappears, user dismisses. No PII exposure (file is already in app-private filesDir), no data loss, no privilege boundary crossed. Agree with deferral.

S4-4.evidence: Manifest entry for CrashReportActivity (android:process=":crash", android:exported="false", android:excludeFromRecents="true") is Task 5 scope per tech-spec Flow A / Decision 5. Tracked as Task 5 handoff. Not re-raising in round 2.
